objective: Prepare native app identity, versioning, Android upload signing, iOS App Store signing, release archives, and upload evidence before App Store or Google Play submission.

officialBasis.name: Android App signing
officialBasis.requirement: Android release builds must be signed before distribution, and App Bundles or APKs should be protected by an upload key or app signing key process.

officialBasis.name: Google Play App Signing
officialBasis.requirement: Google Play can manage and protect the app signing key while developers sign uploads with an upload key.

officialBasis.name: Apple App Store provisioning profile
officialBasis.requirement: App Store distribution requires an App Store provisioning profile tied to the app identifier and signing certificate.

officialBasis.name: App Store Connect upload builds
officialBasis.requirement: iOS builds must be archived, signed, uploaded, processed, and selected in App Store Connect before review.

officialBasis.name: Capacitor configuration
officialBasis.requirement: The native app identity, app name, webDir, and release server behavior must match the Capacitor production configuration.

releaseVersion.versionPolicy: Increase Android versionCode and iOS CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION for every store upload; update marketing version only when the public release version changes.

signingAssets.secureDestination: android/release-signing.properties on a secure build machine or CI secret store
Apple Developer account, macOS keychain, or CI signing secret store
Apple Developer account, Xcode signing, or CI signing secret store

requirements: Android SDK path is set through ANDROID_HOME or ANDROID_SDK_ROOT.
JDK 17 or newer is used for Android Gradle builds.
Gradle can produce a release Android App Bundle.
macOS with current Xcode is available.
Apple Developer team and App Store signing are configured.
Xcode archive can be signed and uploaded.
Install Android build on a physical device or release-grade emulator.
Install iOS build on simulator or device through Xcode/TestFlight flow.
Capture store screenshots after native build validation.

buildCommands.purpose: Validate native signing handoff files, app identity, no-secret rules, and release blockers.
Build web assets and sync them into native projects.
Check Capacitor config, native project baseline, Android SDK/JDK, and iOS Xcode readiness.
Create the Android release App Bundle on Windows after SDK, JDK 17, and signing values are ready.

buildCommands.command: xcodebuild archive -workspace ios/App/App.xcworkspace -scheme App -configuration Release -archivePath build/App.xcarchive
buildCommands.purpose: Create an iOS archive on macOS after Apple signing is configured.

requiredEvidence: Real keystore, p12, mobileprovision, IPA, archive, and signing properties are ignored by git.
Real passwords are stored in password manager or CI secrets.
Android SDK and JDK 17+ are installed.
Upload keystore or CI signing secret reference exists outside the repository.
Release App Bundle is generated and can be uploaded to Play Console.
Apple Developer account and team are selected.
App Store provisioning profile and distribution signing are configured.
Archive is uploaded and processed in App Store Connect.

doNotCommit: Apple p12 certificates or private keys
